cases.title: Local diagnostics survive process restart within bounded retention
expected: Structured diagnostic records are flushed to a Node-owned local directory and remain readable after restart
Retention is bounded and cleanup cannot escape the diagnostic root
Logging failure degrades diagnostics without preventing canonical project recovery

cases.title: Diagnostic output excludes private content and credentials
expected: Prompts, responses, hidden reasoning, tokens, secret environment values and unrestricted filenames do not enter generic logs
Allowed correlation and error fields remain sufficient to join one operation
A redaction regression fails the test with the sensitive field class rather than echoing its value

cases.title: Abnormal previous exit is distinguishable from clean shutdown
expected: Startup records whether the previous supervised process reached clean shutdown
A crash marker identifies component and last durable phase without claiming an unavailable root cause
The next clean shutdown clears only the matching lifecycle marker

cases.title: M02 workspace contracts extend the accepted protocol additively
expected: Project, workspace, change, test, artifact and checkpoint messages expose stable identity, revision and terminal error state
Mutating commands preserve command and correlation identity and do not claim completion on admission
M01 clients remain wire compatible and generated Rust and TypeScript clients match the sources

cases.title: Empty and existing local projects register durably
expected: Creating an empty project creates only the selected directory, durable project record and direct session
Adding an existing folder preserves its contents and detects repository and instruction metadata
Canonical path aliases cannot create duplicate project identity and restart restores the same registration

cases.title: Restricted projects cannot execute or self-elevate
expected: Unknown imported folders start Restricted and may be statically inspected
Project instructions, model output and files cannot grant Trusted-Bounded access or expand roots
An explicit user grant enables only the documented bounded execution scope and remains revocable

cases.title: Missing project locations preserve metadata and block execution
expected: A disappeared folder changes the project to Missing without deleting sessions or metadata
Read or execution commands fail with an actionable missing-location error
Locate or rebind validates identity and trust-sensitive changes before returning to Ready

cases.title: Project instruction changes invalidate the effective instruction fingerprint
expected: Relevant AGENTS or provider instruction changes produce a new fingerprint and observable event
The next provider turn receives the rebuilt effective instruction set
Instructions remain guidance inside the granted workspace and never permission authority

cases.title: File changes and tests bind to one exact workspace snapshot
expected: A mutation is admitted against a stable base revision and produces a new revision or an explicit conflict
Diff, command and test receipts name the revision they observed
Changing any tracked input marks earlier verification stale instead of preserving a false green state

cases.title: Filesystem authority blocks escapes and ungranted roots
expected: Canonicalization prevents dot segments, symlinks, junctions and case aliases from escaping granted roots
Read and write permissions are enforced independently outside the model
Denied attempts produce receipts and do not partially modify either path

cases.title: Concurrent human edits are detected without silent overwrite
expected: A file changed after the base snapshot rejects a blind replacement
Unrelated human changes remain intact when an agent change is applied or reverted
The conflict exposes base, current and proposed identities for retry, merge or cancellation

cases.title: Checkpoint comparison and restore survive interrupted mutation
expected: A checkpoint records base, touched paths, artifacts, effects and provider continuation before consequential mutation
Restart reconstructs committed, unapplied, partially applied or recovery-required state
Restore stops on external divergence and never claims to undo completed external effects

cases.title: Test receipts prove the exact verified project version
expected: Receipt includes command identity, workspace revision, start and finish, exit classification and bounded evidence handles
Pass, fail, timeout, cancelled and spawn failure remain distinct
A receipt cannot be attached to a newer workspace revision without an explicit stale marker

cases.title: Project command cancellation is scoped and terminal
expected: Stop targets one supervised process tree and waits for a bounded terminal acknowledgement
Late output cannot rewrite terminal state
Failure to prove process termination becomes recovery-required rather than cancelled

cases.title: Project commands enforce trust and resource policy outside the agent
expected: Restricted project commands are denied before process spawn
Cwd, executable, network, environment and resource scopes are revalidated by Node
Project text and provider output cannot bypass a denied scope

cases.title: Only declared project outputs become truthful artifacts
expected: Collected artifacts name a real existing output, source command and workspace revision
Missing, oversized, escaped and unsupported outputs are reported honestly
The UI hides empty artifact sections rather than rendering placeholders

cases.title: Local Git worktrees pin base and clean up explicitly
expected: Repository discovery records exact root, head, branch, dirty state and relevant untracked facts
An isolated workspace uses a unique branch and worktree pinned to the declared base without shared stash
Cleanup removes only a proven inactive worktree and preserves uncertain or unintegrated work

cases.title: Git integration reports stale base and conflicts without destructive automation
expected: A moved target branch marks reviewed changes stale before integration
Conflicts preserve both sides and require an explicit resolution path
No force push, shared-branch rewrite or remote publication occurs implicitly

cases.title: Codex changes and verifies a disposable real project
expected: The ChatGPT-subscription Codex runtime reads the selected disposable project, changes the requested file and runs the requested focused check
Normalized activity, diff and test evidence retain one project, session, provider turn and workspace revision chain
The canary contains no private repository content and is excluded from credential-free CI

cases.title: Codex project work restores without replaying committed effects
expected: Restart restores provider continuation, workspace revision and durable effect receipts independently
Already committed file or Git effects are reconciled rather than blindly replayed
Unavailable continuation preserves recoverable project evidence and reports semantic drift if rehydration is required

cases.title: Project review design receives explicit owner approval
expected: An editable Figma prototype uses real M02 state fixtures and the accepted monochrome Mica direction
Files, diffs, tests, artifacts, checkpoints, correction and rollback are understandable without raw logs
The owner approves or requests changes before renderer implementation begins

cases.title: Desktop project creation and import use real Node state
expected: Projects header creates a project while each project and Recent create chats in the correct scope
Create empty project and Add existing folder complete through stable commands and authoritative watches
Missing, Restricted, pending and failed states are visible and no local fixture is presented as live

cases.title: Desktop renders real diff test artifact and checkpoint evidence
expected: Each visible changed file, diff, test, artifact and checkpoint comes from the active Node snapshot
Stale, failed, cancelled, conflicted and recovery-required evidence is not styled as accepted or green
Empty resource sections remain absent and all rendered resources open the matching real viewer

cases.title: Project review is keyboard and assistive-technology operable
expected: Files, changes, review comments, accept, correction and rollback have keyboard and screen-reader paths
No required operation depends only on hover, color, drag or pointer precision
Streaming updates preserve focus and announce bounded meaningful state changes

cases.title: Review correction and rollback preserve exact version context
expected: A correction references the reviewed file or hunk revision and continues in the same project session
Outdated review context is marked stale before a new mutation
Revert hunk, revert file and restore checkpoint affect only proven agent-owned changes or stop on conflict

cases.title: One real project completes the interactive M02 workflow
expected: The user imports a disposable repository, grants bounded trust, asks Codex for a small change and sees ordered progress
The resulting diff and focused test refer to the same workspace revision
The user requests one correction and then either accepts the exact result or restores the prior checkpoint

cases.title: M02 failure matrix remains diagnosable and recoverable
expected: Injected missing folder, stale file, denied path, spawn failure, failed test, cancellation, process exit, Git conflict and cleanup failure each reach a distinct terminal or recovery-required state
Restart preserves canonical evidence and never replays a committed mutation blindly
A privacy-safe diagnostic summary identifies the failing layer, operation and recommended next step

cases.title: Owner accepts one understandable native project workflow
expected: The owner can state what the agent changed and whether tests apply without reading raw technical logs
Correction, accept and rollback choices are clear and operate on the intended revision
The native Files and Changes flow matches the approved visual checkpoint and remaining limitations are explicit
